title: University Spotlight: Caltech — Virtual Tour Guide
excerpt: An in-depth guide for parents on Caltech (California Institute of Technology) — ranked #7 globally (QS 2027) — covering CBSE, State Board and Cambridge A Level admission requirements, the STEM-only core curriculum, the critical financial-aid rule for international students, and an honest fit conversation. The tenth edition completes the QS global top 10.

title: University Spotlight: ETH Zurich — Virtual Tour Guide
excerpt: An in-depth guide for parents on ETH Zurich — ranked #8 globally and #1 in continental Europe (QS 2027) — covering the two honest pathways for Indian students (German-taught Bachelor's and English-taught Master's), ETH's remarkable affordability, the ESOP scholarship, and Swiss visa rules.

title: Two Numbers That Should Keep Us Awake: 69 Percent and Seven
excerpt: India's own think-tank confirms it: 69% of promoted Class V children cannot do basic division, and a landmark survey found 93% of teenagers can name only about seven careers. A reflection on the EducationWorld editorial and the NITI Aayog report — one a crisis of foundation, the other a crisis of imagination, and why only the second is squarely within a counsellor's reach.

title: The Rupee, the Visa, and the Rethink
excerpt: India now sends over 1.2 million students abroad a year — but a weak rupee and tightening visas have quietly changed the maths. A calmer, numbers-first reading of the BBC's 'rethink' story: what's really shifting, why demand is re-routing (not collapsing), and the three questions every family should ask before committing.

title: The Cartoon Ape That Cost a Million Dollars
excerpt: Justin Bieber paid $1.3M for a cartoon ape; by 2026 it was worth about 1% of that. A reflection on the NFT bubble, the greater fool theory, and the one hard data skill it should teach every student — separating real innovation from the hype wrapped around it.

title: Germany: Undergraduate Admissions
excerpt: A counselling-ready guide to Germany undergraduate admissions in 2026 — the four pathways after Class 12, what 'free' tuition really costs, the new 70% Class 12 rule, APS, the blocked account, funding, and a Cambridge A-Level edge. Distilled from a Future Pathways session and fact-checked figure by figure.

title: University Spotlight: UCL — Virtual Tour Guide
excerpt: An in-depth guide for parents on University College London — ranked #8 globally and in the QS top 10 for the 15th consecutive year (QS 2027) — covering CBSE and Cambridge A Level admission requirements, UCL's London advantage, research strengths, and financial support for Indian students.

title: University Spotlight: Stanford — Virtual Tour Guide
excerpt: An in-depth guide for parents on Stanford University — ranked #2 globally (tied with ICL, QS 2027) — covering CBSE and Cambridge A Level admission requirements, Stanford's holistic admissions process, need-blind financial aid, and the unique Silicon Valley advantage for Indian students.

title: University Spotlight: Cambridge — Virtual Tour Guide
excerpt: An in-depth guide for parents on the University of Cambridge — ranked #6 globally (QS 2027) and THE joint #3 — covering CBSE and Cambridge A Level admission requirements, the supervision system, UCAS process, admissions tests (STEP, ENGAA, TMUA), and the unique Cambridge application for Indian students.

title: University Spotlight: Harvard — Virtual Tour Guide
excerpt: An in-depth guide for parents on Harvard University — ranked #5 globally (QS 2027) — covering CBSE and Cambridge A Level admission requirements, Harvard's holistic admissions process, need-blind financial aid, and what makes a competitive Harvard application.

title: University Spotlight: Oxford — Virtual Tour Guide
excerpt: An in-depth guide for parents on the University of Oxford — ranked #4 globally (QS 2027) and THE #1 for 10 consecutive years — covering CBSE and Cambridge A Level admission requirements, the tutorial system, UCAS process, admissions tests, and interview preparation for Indian students.

title: University Spotlight: Imperial College London — Virtual Tour Guide
excerpt: An in-depth guide for parents on Imperial College London — ranked #2 globally and #1 in the UK for STEM (QS 2027) — covering CBSE and Cambridge A Level admission requirements, IELTS waivers, fees, scholarships, and career outcomes in science and engineering.

title: University Spotlight: MIT — Virtual Tour Guide
excerpt: An in-depth guide for parents on MIT — ranked #1 globally for 15 consecutive years (QS 2027) — covering CBSE and Cambridge A Level admission requirements, SAT strategy, need-blind financial aid, and how to build a competitive MIT application.

title: University Spotlight: NUS — Virtual Tour Guide
excerpt: An in-depth guide for parents on NUS — ranked #10 globally and #1 in Asia (QS 2027) — covering CBSE and Cambridge A Level admission requirements, the MOE Tuition Grant, NUS International Undergraduate Scholarship, and why Singapore is one of the best destinations for Indian students.

title: Data — The New Currency of the Modern World
excerpt: Session notes from a 75-minute Future Pathways talk at TIPS Erode — what data is, how every profession is now data-driven, and why data careers that need zero coding are among the best opportunities open to Humanities, Arts and Commerce students today.

title: Process to Outcomes
excerpt: An educator's reflections on how AI is shifting the unit of value from process to outcome — and why the same shift matters just as much in classrooms and counselling rooms as it does in boardrooms.

title: Chemical Engineering & Why Research Matters
excerpt: Key takeaways from a session by Prof. Kannan Kandasamy at TIPS School Erode — what chemical engineering is, why the world urgently needs it, career scope in India and abroad, and a seven-step research roadmap any IGCSE student can start today.

title: Inside IIT Bombay: What the Open House Told JEE Advanced Qualifiers
excerpt: Key takeaways from IIT Bombay's official JEE Advanced Open House — flexible curriculum, open labs, AI + X philosophy, global exchange, and a first-year playbook for making the most of four years at India's flagship engineering institution.

title: Built for This: A Guide to Thriving as a Student
excerpt: What you believe shapes what you do — and what you do shapes who you become. A practical guide to the beliefs, habits, and mindset shifts that help students go from getting by to truly thriving.

title: Game Plan for College: How MAE Sports & Education Helps Student-Athletes Find Their Best Fit and Earn Athletic Scholarships
excerpt: Key takeaways from a May 2026 ISP webinar by Macarena Aguirre — how the US athletic scholarship process works, what counsellors need to know, and how to support student-athletes from any sport level through MAE Sports & Education.

title: The Psychology of Career Decisions: Why Students Don't Choose Careers Rationally
excerpt: Key takeaways from a TEDxWesleyanU talk by Sharon Belden Castonguay — why career decisions are mostly irrational, how the social surround limits student choices, and what it means to treat identity as a script rather than an equation.

title: Critical Thinking in Career Counselling: Using the RED Model to Strengthen Your Practice
excerpt: A structured guide for career counsellors on applying the RED model — Recognise Assumptions, Evaluate Arguments, Draw Conclusions — to sharpen your own practice and build critical thinking skills in students.

title: Making the Most of a Campus Visit: The 5Ws for Counsellors
excerpt: Key takeaways from an IACAC webinar on campus visits — a practical 5W framework covering preparation, observation, note-taking, and follow-through for school counsellors.

title: UCAS Personal Statements: What University Admissions Truly Want
excerpt: Key takeaways from a Unifrog webinar on UCAS personal statements — what UK university admissions teams truly want, common pitfalls to avoid, and the Sutton Trust research that shows why counsellor support matters.

title: Relevance of AI in Current Industry Dynamics: 5 Takeaways for Counsellors
excerpt: Five key takeaways from the Salesforce Great Asia AI Summit and an HBR Ideacast podcast — grounding counsellors in the real-world AI conversation so they can answer students' hardest questions about computer science and data science.

title: Psychometric Tests: What Students and Counsellors Need to Know
excerpt: Key takeaways from a Unifrog webinar on psychometric testing — helping counsellors prepare students for the assessments used by companies like Nestlé and Rolls-Royce, and the direct link to Unifrog's self-assessment tools.

title: Econometrics & Career Pathways: A Guide for High School Career Counsellors
excerpt: A practical guide for career counsellors on surfacing econometrics and data career pathways early — linking subject choices to global job roles, earning potential, and free tools students can start using today.

title: Econometrics in the Classroom: Key Insights for Economics & Mathematics Teachers
excerpt: Key takeaways from a FutureWise webinar on econometrics — helping Economics and Mathematics teachers connect classroom theory to real-world data careers through correlation, regression, and the power of Y = MX + B.

title: You Are Your Only Competition
excerpt: The most successful people aren't trying to beat others — they compete only with who they were yesterday. Here's why self-competition is the most powerful mindset shift you can make.

title: Getting Started with Cambridge Curriculum
excerpt: An introduction to the Cambridge International curriculum framework, its core principles, and how it transforms student learning outcomes in international schools.

title: Artificial Intelligence in Education: What Teachers Need to Know
excerpt: A practical guide for educators on how AI tools are reshaping classrooms, and how to harness them responsibly for better learning outcomes.

title: 10 Career Counselling Tips Every High School Student Should Know
excerpt: From building a strong academic profile to choosing the right university pathway, these ten tips will help high school students navigate their future with confidence.

title: Scrolling Vs Studying: A Short Guide to Dopamine Detox
excerpt: Why does studying feel like climbing Everest while scrolling feels effortless? The answer lies in dopamine — and here's a practical detox plan to reset your brain's reward system.

title: Your Digital Stuff is a Mess? Let's Fix It!
excerpt: Folders everywhere, missing files, inbox overflowing — your digital life is a mess and it's costing you time and peace of mind. Here's a simple, actionable plan to organise it once and for all.
